title: teucrium divaricatum subsp. canescens
intro: Teucrium divaricatum subsp. canescens, also known as the shrubby germander, is a subspecies of the Teucrium divaricatum plant, a flowering perennial native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Teucrium divaricatum subsp. canescens include relief from digestive issues, such as indigestion and diarrhea, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory problems like bronchitis. Teucrium divaricatum subsp. canescens can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine.
